articles.title: Pound Sterling edges higher as traders price in two BoE rate cut odds
articles.expand: GBP/USD inches higher after registering modest losses in the previous day, trading around 1.3230 during the Asian hours on Friday. Trading activity may remain subdued due to the Good Friday holiday.

articles.title: Crude Prices Soar as Iran War Keeps Strait of Hormuz Closed
articles.expand: May WTI crude oil (CLK26 ) on Thursday closed up +11.42 (+11.41%), and May RBOB gasoline (RBK26 ) closed up +0.1966 (+6.36%). Crude oil and gasoline prices rallied sharply on Thursday, with crude posting a 3.5-week high. Crude prices are soaring after President Trump pledged more aggressive action against Iran...

articles.title: Dollar Climbs on Fears of a Prolonged Iran War
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Thursday rose by +0.36%. Thursday's decline in equity markets boosted liquidity demand for the dollar. Also, Wednesday night's comments from President Trump that the Iran war could escalate have boosted demand for the dollar as a safe-haven. The dollar added to its gains on...

articles.title: Japanese Yen flat lines amid persistent tensions in the Middle East
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair trades on a flat note near 159.60 during the Asian trading hours on Friday. Trading volumes are likely to be thin due to the Good Friday holiday. All eyes will be on the US March Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report, which will be published later on Friday.

articles.title: EUR/USD posts modest gains to near 1.1550, traders brace for US NFP data
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair posts modest gains around 1.1540 during the early Asian session on Friday. Trading volumes are likely to be thin due to the Good Friday holiday. The US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report for March will take center stage later on Friday.

articles.title: Fed’s Goolsbee warns oil surge risks lifting inflation expectations
articles.expand: Goolsbee warns the oil shock risks derailing disinflation, with duration key. Rising gasoline prices could lift inflation expectations, complicating Fed policy just as inflation was expected to ease. ICYMI, Thursday oil price jump: WTI crude oil up $11.25 to $111.38 Summary: Goolsbee flags oil surge as “pretty serious,” with duration key for inflat...

articles.title: NZD/USD weakens as Iran war tensions rise
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair is trading around the 0.5710 region, maintaining a bearish tone amid heightened geopolitical tensions and risk aversion, as the US Dollar (USD) strengthens.

articles.expand: WTI and Brent Oil update: Market skepticism grows as President Trump’s optimistic rhetoric clashes with a 14% spike in WTI prices. articles.title: AUDUSD finds sellers against the 200 hour MA
articles.expand: The AUDUSD slipped below its 100-hour moving average at 0.68846 during the Asian session, and early attempts to hold below that level added a more bearish technical tone. However, downside momentum stalled near 0.6858, signaling a lack of follow-through from sellers. As equities recovered, the pair rebounded and moved back above the 100-hour moving...

articles.title: Gold slides over 2% as Trump extends Iran operation, lifts USD bids
articles.expand: Gold price recoils by some 2% as the US Dollar rises after US President Donald Trump revealed that the war on Iran would last from two to three weeks, pouring cold water on hopes for a quick resolution to the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: Why are non-farm payrolls being released on Good Friday? Here's what's open and what isn't
articles.expand: Tomorrow is one of those rare calendar collisions: the March non-farm payrolls report is released at 8:30 am ET on Good Friday — a day when the stock market is closed. Good Friday has been a NYSE holiday almost every year since 1864. It's the only stock market holiday that isn't also a federal holiday. The date moves around because it's tied to the...

articles.title: Iran war jeopardizes global food prices
articles.expand: You think soaring Oil prices are the main problem? Are you worried about the impact of energy prices on inflation? Think again.

articles.title: ECB's Villeroy says it is too soon to say when rates could rise
articles.expand: The European Central Bank's next interest rate move will very likely be ​an increase although it is still ‌too early to say when it will start hiking, ECB policymaker Francois Villeroy de Galhau said ​on Thursday.

articles.title: Malaysia Airlines operator posts higher earnings in 2025 but Middle East conflict clouds outlook
articles.expand: KUALA LUMPUR ― Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG), the operator of national carrier Malaysia Airlines, reported higher profits in 2025 but warned on Thursday that market volatility caused by the conflict in the Middle East may weigh on its performance this year. The monthlong Iran war has shaken the global aviation industry, with a sharp surge in jet fu...

articles.title: Labour's latest pension shakeup labelled 'immoral' as taxpayers foot £3billion bill
articles.expand: Labour ministers have saddled British taxpayers with a £3billion bill after seizing control of the Atomic Weapons Establishment pension fund, despite the scheme holding sufficient assets to meet 92 per cent of its obligations independently. The decision, buried within documents published following the Budget, will transfer responsibility for paying...

articles.title: Top 5 High-Yield ASX 200 Dividend Stocks April 2026 Offer Income Amid RBA Rate Volatility
articles.expand: SYDNEY — Investors seeking reliable income in a volatile interest rate environment are turning to high-yield dividend stocks within the S&P/ASX 200 Index as the Reserve Bank of Australia holds the cash rate at 4.1% following recent hikes, making dividend yields from established companies an attractive alternative to term deposits and bonds.

articles.title: Euro area inflation rises on energy shock, core trends stay limited
articles.expand: Euro area inflation rose to 2.5% mainly due to higher energy prices driven by tensions in Iran, while core inflation remained subdued. Price pressures have not broadly spread across the economy, but second-round effects may emerge over time. The European Central Bank is likely to respond cautiously, with limited need for aggressive rate hikes.

articles.title: Swiss CPI Rises to 0.3% yoy, But Underlying Inflation Stays Soft
articles.expand: Swiss inflation remained subdued in March, with CPI rising 0.2% mom, below expectations of 0.5% mom. Annual inflation edged up from 0.1% yoy to 0.3% yoy, also missing forecasts of 0.5% yoy. The data highlights a continued lack of underlying price pressure despite some pickup in energy-related components. The breakdown shows a clear divergence. Impo...

articles.title: Switzerland March CPI +0.3% vs +0.5% y/y expected
articles.expand: Prior +0.1% Core CPI +0.4% y/y Prior +0.4% Even with the surge in energy prices, the impact on Swiss price pressures is less profound that what we saw with the Eurozone. Even the monthly estimate was +0.2%, missing on expectations of +0.5%. As such, the jump in headline annual inflation was also less than expected. This reaffirms that even with ene...

Equities and AUD/USD weakened as bearish momentum resumed, while the US dollar strengthened. articles.title: Investors rush in hope that War resolution isn't an April Fool's – North American Mid-Week Market Update
articles.expand: Mid-Week update for North-American Markets – The narrative has changed for Markets, easing risk-sentiment for Stock Indexes and dragging both the US Dollar and Canadian Dollar. Taking a close look at NA index and currency performance combined with a USD/CAD intraday chart to spot what's next for American Markets.

articles.title: Euro comes out swinging: Can the "Trump Reversal" sustain EUR/USD's upside bias?
articles.expand: EUR/USD has surged towards 1.1600 due to "war optimism" following US President Donald Trump's comments and the hawkish stance of the ECB, which has created a yield differential favoring the Euro. The "Trump Reversal" seems to hold the key.

We will have to act if appropriate, but tackling source of energy price shock is most important If high energy prices and supply disruptions persist, i...

articles.title: Eurozone PMI Manufacturing Finalized at 45-Month High But War-Fueled Inflation Clouds Outlook
articles.expand: Eurozone PMI Manufacturing was finalized at 51.6 in March, up from 50.8, marking a 45-month high and signaling continued, albeit modest, expansion in the sector. articles.title: Is risk-on, back on? The AUDUSD is higher but it is not running quickly to the upside.
articles.expand: The AUDUSD typically trades as a classic risk-on/risk-off barometer. When equities are under pressure and yields are pushing higher, the pair tends to drift lower as traders reduce risk exposure. Conversely, when stocks firm and yields ease, AUDUSD often benefits from a more constructive, risk-on backdrop. Today, the pair is higher by about 0.62%, ...

articles.title: Gold (XAU/USD) rallies 3%, eyes acceptance above the $4600/oz handle for bullish momentum to continue
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) is showing a technical recovery, breaking $4,600/oz as escalating Middle East tensions and a shifting correlation with rising Oil prices boost the "inflation hedge" narrative. Will the bullish momentum continue?

However, that momentum faded quickly, and buying interest dried up, leading to a reversal. The pair has since rotated back toward unchanged on the day, currently trading near 1.3180 after reaching a high of 1.3264...

articles.title: USDJPY moves further from the 160.00 level and cracks below the 200 hour MA target
articles.expand: The USDJPY has rotated lower after failing above the 160.00 level, a key psychological and technical barrier. Recall, the pair broke above that level on Friday, reaching a high of 160.455, but the breakout lacked follow-through. Yesterday, the price moved lower and tested an upward sloping trendline, where buyers stepped in and pushed the pair back...
